event_summary: Three people died and at least ten tested positive for Andes hantavirus in an outbreak aboard the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius; by May 17, 2026, a Canadian passenger became the first confirmed case outside Europe, triggering hospital isolation in British Columbia.
tldr: Headline framing is overwhelmingly HEALTH_SAFETY — positive tests, quarantine logistics, and case-count updates dominate across 18 of 25 buckets. The most notable divergence is the USA bucket (The Intercept): its headline names a CDC notification failure rather than a case update, the only title in the corpus to frame the outbreak as an accountability story. Germany and the Balkans uniquely pushed a COVID-comparison-anxiety angle, with Turkish headlines amplifying pandemic-era fear. Spain's El Pais headline zoomed out to climate change and zoonotic risk as the broadest contextual frame. Russia_native's title about the embassy receiving no requests is the most isolated and most parochial headline in the corpus.

evidence.quote: hantavirus-affected cruise to be quarantined for three weeks
B.C. couple in hospital isolation after testing positive
No evidence of Hantavirus in Iran: health ministry
CDC Didn't Tell New York About Resident on Hantavirus-Plagued Cruise
Vietnam, Cambodia strengthen airport screening for hantavirus
France tightens health surveillance on deadly Hantavirus
hantavirus outbreak revives COVID-era fears worldwide
Warum das Hantavirus nicht mit Corona vergleichbar ist
A Scientist's Close Call with Hantavirus Aboard the M.V. Hondius

isolation_top.note: Russian-language Kommersant headline about the embassy receiving no requests from a Russian crew member — parochial angle invisible in every other bucket.
Korea Herald headline frames the outbreak as a moment to spotlight South Korea's founding role in hantavirus discovery — national-pride angle absent elsewhere.
German-language explainer headlines on pandemic terminology and the COVID-comparison angle isolate semantically from the English case-count dominant.
El Pais headline zooms to 10,000 zoonotic viruses and climate change — a macro contextual frame absent from all other coverage.
Serbian title in Latin script explicitly refutes a COVID-19 comparison — language confounding combined with genre divergence lowers cosine similarity.

silences.what_they_covered_instead: La Nacion's headline is about birds at a landfill — tangentially tied to hantavirus origin in its body but no frame is detectable from the title alone.
France 24 Spanish ran a fact-check headline on COVID-vaccine misinformation; RT Africa covered the ship leaving Cape Verde — neither applies a disease-frame to current case counts.
Fox News ran a WHO Ebola emergency headline entirely unrelated to hantavirus; only The Intercept covered the outbreak with an accountability angle.
The New Yorker's first headline was about high-school debate, unrelated; the second was a first-person scientist narrative departing from news-report genre.

bottom_line: Headlines across 25 buckets converge on the same HEALTH_SAFETY frame of quarantine logistics and positive-test updates, with Germany, Turkey, and the Balkans as the only outlets using headlines to directly invoke COVID-19 comparisons. The US bucket's accountability-frame headline (CDC failure) is unique in attributing blame to an institution rather than tracking a case number — the sharpest headline-vs-body divergence to watch in the sensationalism index.
